SUPERSEDED (2026-07-22) — see BOUNDARY_CUT_EVIDENCE_v4.html. v4 rebuilds the complete cemetery footprint after Joel found the single OSM outline clips real cemetery ground (north notch + northeast): full OSM sweep + drone-content cross-check, delivering v4a (48.25 ac, == v3) and v4b (50.37 ac). This v2 page is history only.

Oakland Cemetery — boundary cut evidence (v2)

Prepared 2026-07-21 · cemetery + full road ring to the far sidewalk, exterior buildings cut

Preparation and evidence only — no gaussians have been cut. This is for your one-look approval.
"in every case, i want the entire road around the cemetery all the way to the sidewalk across the street from the cemetery, but no buildings outside the cemetery walls kept."
all sides
continuous street band, wall → far sidewalk
Memorial S · Oakland Ave W · Boulevard E · rail+road N
33
exterior buildings cut out
footprints removed cleanly (holes in the band)
16 / 20 / 0
cells inside / partial / outside
(v1 was 15 / 20 / 1)
0.23 m
georegistration residual (median)
p95 0.44 m · cemetery 48.3 ac, within 0.7%

The decision figure — 6×6 grid in model coordinates

Green fill = the v2 keep region (cemetery + street band + rail bundle). Red hatch = the 33 exterior buildings cut out. Cell shading: green wholly inside, orange partially cut (edge ring), gray wholly outside. The dark green line is the cemetery wall.

Geographic sanity — real-world coordinates, north up

The green keep region rings the cemetery: the full rail bundle and the road beyond it on the north, and the streets out to the far building line on the south, east and west. Grey = neighbourhood buildings; red hatch = the ones cut out because they sit outside the walls.

The street band lifted every edge tile versus v1: the south row went from a 4–11% sliver to a 14–26% road strip, the west column and corners all gained, and tile 5_5 — the only wholly-cut tile in v1 — is now a 3% rail clip. The 16 central tiles are the solid cemetery interior.

How the band was built

  • Roads — every perimeter road buffered to its far sidewalk (16 m arterials, ~11 m residential, ~7 m service), clipped to within 45 m of the wall so side streets stop at the far corner.
  • Railway — the full adjacent bundle (all tracks within 150 m), buffered and merged, clipped to the frontage +100 m; plus roads within 28 m of the rail to reach the street beyond it.
  • Buildings — every footprint outside the walls unioned and subtracted, cutting cleanly at the footprint.

One dial if you want it tighter or looser: the 45 m road ring. Everything else is a clean ring by construction — no width or bridge choices remain.

Superseded v1 figures (single 30 m railway corridor, no road ring) — click to expand

Files (all in this folder)

  • boundary_keep_polygon_v2.json — the v2 keep region in model coordinates (polygons with holes; the cut stage's input)
  • cell_table_v2.json — full 36-tile classification with bounds
  • oakland_roads.geojson, oakland_footways.geojson, oakland_buildings.geojson, oakland_cemetery.geojson, oakland_railway.geojson — OSM geometry (+ raw responses)
  • georegistration_transform.json — the world↔model transform and residuals
  • fig_A_model_grid_v2.png, fig_B_world_context_v2.png — v2 figures (v1 figures kept, superseded)
  • BOUNDARY_CUT_EVIDENCE.md — the written version of this page
